title: Ebola Crisis Deepens as Death Toll Passes 200
subtitle: International funding struggles to keep pace with the rapid spread of the virus in the Congo.

summary.en: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is worsening quickly. Official reports show that the number of deaths has now passed 200. Cases have increased by nearly 40 percent in just one week. While the U.S. CDC has provided emergency funding, international aid is arriving slowly. Experts warn that more support is needed to stop the virus from spreading further.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國的伊波拉疫情正迅速惡化。官方報告顯示，死亡人數已超過兩百人，且病例在短短一週內激增近百分之四十。儘管美國疾病管制與預防中心已提供緊急資金，但國際援助到位緩慢。專家警告，必須提供更多支援才能阻止病毒進一步擴散。

paragraphs.en: The situation regarding the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has become more serious. According to MedPage Today, the number of confirmed deaths from the virus has now passed 200. This update follows a worrying trend where the number of cases has increased by nearly 40 percent in just one week. Ebola is a very dangerous and often deadly virus that causes severe bleeding and fever. Because it spreads through contact with the bodily fluids of infected people, it is very difficult to control once it begins to move through a community.
paragraphs.zh: 剛果民主共和國的伊波拉病毒疫情已變得更加嚴峻。根據醫學新聞網站 MedPage Today 的報導，該病毒的確診死亡人數現已突破兩百人。這項最新數據顯示出一種令人擔憂的趨勢，即病例數在短短一週內激增了近百分之四十。伊波拉是一種極度危險且常致命的病毒，會導致嚴重出血與發燒。由於它透過接觸感染者的體液傳播，一旦開始在社區內擴散，便極難控制。

paragraphs.en: International health organizations are working hard to manage the crisis, but they face significant challenges. The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) recently activated $107 million in emergency funding to support the global response to the outbreak. This money is intended to help local health workers, provide medical supplies, and improve tracking of the disease. However, the Africa CDC has noted that the actual amount of money received is much lower than what was promised. They reported that less than 10 percent of the money pledged by international donors has actually arrived to help the people on the ground.
paragraphs.zh: 國際衛生組織正努力應對這場危機，但面臨著重大挑戰。美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）近期啟動了一筆一億零七百萬美元的緊急資金，以支持全球對抗此次疫情。這筆款項旨在協助當地醫護人員、提供醫療物資並改善疾病追蹤工作。然而，非洲疾病管制與預防中心指出，實際收到的金額遠低於承諾數額。他們表示，國際捐助者所承諾的資金中，實際到位並用於協助當地民眾的比例不到百分之十。

paragraphs.en: This gap between promised support and actual funding is a major concern for health experts. When money does not arrive on time, it becomes harder to buy the necessary equipment, such as protective gear for doctors and nurses, or to set up treatment centers. The delay in funding can make the situation much worse, as the virus does not wait for international agreements to be completed. Without quick access to these resources, local teams are struggling to keep up with the fast-growing number of patients who need care.
paragraphs.zh: 這種承諾支援與實際撥款之間的落差，是衛生專家極為憂心的問題。當資金未能及時到位，採購必要的設備（例如醫護人員的防護裝備）或設立治療中心就會變得更加困難。資金延遲可能導致情況惡化，因為病毒不會等待國際協議完成。若無法迅速取得這些資源，當地團隊將難以應付快速增加的病患需求。

paragraphs.en: This current health crisis is part of a larger, ongoing struggle to manage infectious diseases in the region. As previously reported, international leaders and groups like the G7 have promised hundreds of millions of euros to assist with medical support and research. While these promises are important, the recent data shows that the virus is moving faster than the aid. The increase in cases by nearly 40 percent in a single week highlights how quickly the situation can get out of control if there is not a fast and effective response.
paragraphs.zh: 目前的健康危機是該地區長期對抗傳染病的一環。如先前報導，國際領袖與七大工業國集團（G7）等組織曾承諾提供數億歐元，以協助醫療支援與研究。儘管這些承諾至關重要，但最新數據顯示病毒擴散的速度已超越了援助的速度。一週內病例數增加近百分之四十，凸顯出若缺乏快速且有效的應對，疫情將會多麼迅速地失控。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the focus remains on stopping the spread of the virus before it reaches more people. Health officials are calling for donors to send the promised funds as soon as possible to ensure that medical teams have what they need. The international community is under pressure to turn their promises into action. If the funding continues to be slow, it will be very difficult to bring the outbreak under control. The lives of many people in the affected areas depend on how quickly the world can respond to this urgent call for help.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，重點仍在於阻止病毒擴散，避免更多人受害。衛生官員呼籲捐助者儘速撥付承諾資金，以確保醫療團隊擁有所需物資。國際社會正面臨將承諾轉化為行動的壓力。若資金撥付持續緩慢，將極難控制疫情。受影響地區許多民眾的性命，取決於全球對這項緊急求援呼籲的反應速度。

paragraphs.en: In addition to the financial challenges, there is also the difficult task of educating local communities about how to stay safe. Many people may not understand how the virus spreads or may be afraid of the treatment centers. Public health workers are working to build trust with local families to ensure that those who are sick get the help they need. This work is just as important as the money, as it helps to prevent the virus from moving from one person to another. The combination of better funding and strong community cooperation is the best hope for stopping the outbreak.
paragraphs.zh: 除了財務挑戰外，教育當地社區如何保持安全也是一項艱鉅任務。許多民眾可能不了解病毒的傳播方式，或對治療中心感到恐懼。公共衛生工作者正致力於與當地家庭建立信任，確保病患能獲得所需的協助。這項工作與資金同樣重要，因為它有助於防止病毒在人與人之間傳播。充足的資金與強大的社區合作，是遏止疫情的最佳希望。

paragraphs.en: As the world watches the situation in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the message from health experts is clear: the time to act is now. Every day that passes without enough support makes the work of doctors and nurses harder and puts more lives at risk. The international community must work together to close the funding gap and provide the resources needed to save lives. Only through a united and fast response can the spread of Ebola be stopped and the safety of the region be restored.
paragraphs.zh: 當全球關注剛果民主共和國的局勢時，衛生專家的訊息很明確：現在就是採取行動的時刻。每一天缺乏足夠支援的過去，都會讓醫護人員的工作更加困難，並使更多生命陷入危險。國際社會必須攜手合作，縮小資金缺口並提供拯救生命所需的資源。唯有透過團結且迅速的應對，才能阻止伊波拉病毒擴散，並恢復該地區的安全。

keyTerms.definitionEn: In the actual location where an event is happening, rather than at a headquarters or from a distance.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 在現場；在實地（指實際執行任務的地方）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的片語，但在新聞中常指第一線人員。文中：They reported that less than 10 percent of the money pledged by international donors has actually arrived to help the people on the ground.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To manage to move or progress at the same speed as something else.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指醫療資源的增加速度趕不上病毒擴散的速度。文中：Without quick access to these resources, local teams are struggling to keep up with the fast-growing number of patients who need care.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To do what one has said they would do; to implement plans.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 將承諾付諸行動；說到做到。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個強調實踐的慣用語，而非字面上的「轉變」。文中：The international community is under pressure to turn their promises into action.

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: Outbreak Kills Over 200; ACIP Investigation Launched; Hackers Demand $75M From Novo

previousArticles.title: Ebola Outbreak Grows as Health Officials Call for Urgent Support
previousArticles.summaryEn: A serious Ebola outbreak is spreading across the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. Health officials report that confirmed cases have increased by nearly 40 percent in just one week. The disease is caused by a rare virus that currently has no approved vaccine. International organizations are now providing emergency funding to help stop the spread. However, experts warn that more money and personnel are required to manage the crisis effectively.

previousArticles.title: International Support Grows as Ebola Outbreak Challenges DRC
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is currently managing a serious Ebola outbreak involving a rare viral strain. International groups, including the EU and G7, have promised nearly 500 million euros to help with medical support and research. Local communities in Bunia are struggling with both the fear of infection and a sharp decline in their daily income. Experts note that the virus is difficult to treat because existing vaccines are not fully effective against this specific strain. Global health authorities continue to emphasize that international cooperation is essential to stop the spread of the disease.

previousArticles.title: Global Response Grows as Ebola Outbreak Spreads in the DRC
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is facing a serious Ebola outbreak that has already caused hundreds of deaths. International leaders at the G7 summit have pledged significant financial support to help contain the virus. The current outbreak involves a rare strain that is difficult to treat with existing medical tools. Local communities in Bunia are struggling with both the health risks and the economic impact of the disease. Experts warn that global cooperation is essential to manage this public health emergency effectively.

quiz.question: What is the current status of the financial aid pledged by international donors to the Africa CDC?

options.A: The full $107 million has been successfully delivered to local health workers.
options.B: Less than 10 percent of the promised funds have actually been received.
options.C: The funding has been delayed by the G7 to prioritize medical research over supplies.
options.D: The amount received is 40 percent higher than what was originally promised.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Africa CDC 指出實際收到的捐款遠低於承諾金額，僅不到承諾總額的 10%。選項 A 錯誤，因為資金並未全數到位；選項 C 錯誤，因為文中並未提到 G7 刻意延遲資金以優先進行研究；選項 D 則將文章提到的「病例增加 40%」與資金混淆，屬於錯誤資訊。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why does the delay in international funding specifically hinder the containment of the Ebola virus?

options.A: It prevents local health workers from traveling to remote areas to find patients.
options.B: It causes local communities to lose trust in the international health organizations.
options.C: It stops the procurement of essential supplies needed to manage patients and prevent transmission.
options.D: It forces doctors and nurses to leave the treatment centers due to a lack of salary.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第三段，資金缺口導致難以購買必要的設備（如防護裝備）或設立治療中心。這些資源對於控制病毒擴散至關重要。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 B 雖然提到建立信任很重要，但資金延遲並非導致不信任的主因；選項 D 關於薪資的問題文中並未提及，屬於過度推論。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'the international community is under pressure to turn their promises into action,' what does the word 'turn' most closely mean?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境下，「turn...into」意指將某種狀態或承諾「轉換」或「轉化」為實際的行動。選項 A (旋轉) 是物理意義上的轉動，不適用於此；選項 C (反轉) 與語意相反；選項 D (導航) 與此句意圖表達的「落實承諾」完全無關。

quiz.question: What is the primary message the author conveys regarding the Ebola outbreak?

options.A: The Ebola virus is becoming resistant to modern medical treatments.
options.B: Local communities are solely responsible for stopping the spread of the virus.
options.C: The virus is spreading primarily because of a lack of scientific research.
options.D: An urgent, coordinated international response is required to provide funding and support to stop the crisis.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章多次強調資金缺口、國際承諾與實際行動之間的落差，並指出唯有國際社會快速合作、落實援助，才能有效控制疫情。選項 A 文中未提及病毒抗藥性；選項 B 忽略了國際援助的重要性；選項 C 錯誤，文章強調的是資源與資金的匱乏，而非缺乏科學研究。
